findings.evidence: GradingInteger.lean:67 `def maslovOℤ`; the proofs rw [maslovOℤ_def, ...]; GradingInteger.lean is 200 lines, longFile limit 1000 (lakefile.toml:20).
findings.fix: Move both theorems into GradingInteger.lean next to maslovOℤ_def/maslovXℤ_def and delete SouthWestCount.lean.
findings.issue: maslovOℤ_eq_card and maslovXℤ_eq_card elaborate maslovOℤ/maslovXℤ, which are defined in GradingInteger.lean (line 67/80); that file imports everything these proofs use and is only 200 lines (limit 1000). They are split into a separate new file instead, against the author's own pattern of co-locating the J-level reductions inside JFunction.lean.

findings.evidence: File docstring: 'The column-index reductions ... live with the J API in ... JFunction; this file applies them to the integer Maslov gradings.' GridPoint.I_def is in JFunction.lean.
findings.fix: If a separate file is kept, name it for its actual content (integer Maslov gradings as column counts); otherwise fold into GradingInteger.lean per the finding above.
findings.issue: Filename is misleading: the southwest count GridPoint.I is defined and developed in JFunction.lean, not here; this file only restates two Maslov gradings as counts, as its own docstring admits.

summary: Imports are clean and justified. But the two integer-Maslov capstones were spun into a new, misleadingly-named file instead of placed with their API in GradingInteger.lean.
